# Office Display Manager — Technical Documentation

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate-docs.mjs. Sections between BEGIN:generated /
     END:generated markers are overwritten on every `npm run build`. Write prose
     ONLY inside BEGIN:manual / END:manual blocks — those are preserved. -->

<!-- BEGIN:manual:overview -->
## Overview

Office Display Manager (ODM) drives office screens (TVs, kiosks) as unattended
slideshows of web URLs. A manager creates a **Slideshow** record, lists the URLs
to rotate, assigns it to a **service account** (the technical user a screen logs
in as), and sets a slide duration and working-hours window. The screen opens a
single permanent URL and plays whatever active slideshow is assigned to its
account — rotating slides in a sandboxed iframe, blanking to a standby clock
outside working hours, and showing an idle card when nothing is assigned.

The whole app is one custom table plus a self-contained HTML player served two
ways: a Scripted REST API and a direct UI page. There are **no custom roles** —
access is ownership-based (creator / assigned service account / platform admin).

> The sections below the app-fact table are **generated** from source by
> `scripts/generate-docs.mjs`. Edit prose only inside the `manual` blocks.
<!-- END:manual:overview -->

<!-- BEGIN:manual:architecture -->
## Architecture notes

**Request paths.** A screen can reach the player two ways, both session-authenticated:

- **Direct UI page** `x_804244_odm_player.do?screen=<user_name>` — `direct: true`,
  so the platform adds no UI16/Polaris chrome. A Jelly page calls the
  `OdmPlayerPage` script include, which `require()`s the server module and returns
  the fully rendered player document (deck + session token injected server-side).
  This is the address-bar entry point; anonymous visitors get the login redirect
  for free.
- **Scripted REST API** under `/api/x_804244_odm/player` — serves the same HTML
  plus a JSON `deck` route the player polls for live updates. REST rejects
  cookie-only calls (X-UserToken CSRF rule), which is why the deck carries a
  session token for the player's `fetch` polling.

**Layering.** Pure logic (`src/server/deck.ts`, `src/client/templates/player.js`)
has no Glide or DOM dependencies, so Jest covers every branch. The thin Glide
layer (`handlers.src.ts`) does record access and enforces the open-time access
rule. `scripts/build-template.mjs` inlines `player.js` into `rotator.html` and
concatenates the pure + handler sources into the self-contained, committed
`src/server/player-routes.ts` — the only module the SDK actually deploys.

**Security model.** Table ACLs gate CRUD by ownership (see the ACL table). The
player's *open-time* check is separate and lives in the handler: a slideshow
opens only for its creator, its assigned service account, a platform admin, or
any slideshow flagged `public`. The record is read with a plain `GlideRecord`
(not `GlideRecordSecure`) precisely so the handler can distinguish "no slideshow"
(idle) from "exists but forbidden" (access-denied). Manager-supplied content is
`<`/`>`-escaped before injection so it can never break out of the
CDATA-wrapped script context.

**Invariants.** At most one active slideshow per service account (enforced by the
`unique active slideshow` business rule) so the player always resolves exactly
one deck per screen. `player_link` is system-maintained by a business rule and
kept read-only on the form by a UI policy.
<!-- END:manual:architecture -->

## Data model

<!-- BEGIN:generated:tables -->
### `x_804244_odm_slideshow` — Slideshow

The only custom table in ODM (SPEC.md Data Model).

| Field | Type | Label | Mandatory | Max | Default | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `name` | String | Name | yes | 100 |  |  |
| `description` | String | Description |  | 1000 |  |  |
| `assigned_account` | Reference | Service account |  |  |  | → sys_user — The technical/service account this slideshow is displayed under. A screen logged in as this account (e.g. via Bomgar) plays this slideshow. The player link points here. |
| `links` | String | Links (comma-separated URLs) |  | 8000 |  | Comma-separated URLs (newlines also work). Literal commas inside a URL must be encoded as %2C. Entries must be absolute (https://...) or root-relative (/...). |
| `slide_duration` | Integer | Slide duration (s) |  |  | `30` | Seconds each slide is shown; applies to every slide in the deck |
| `hours_start` | String | Working hours start (HH:MM) |  | 5 | `07:00` | 24h format. Invalid or empty window = always on |
| `hours_end` | String | Working hours end (HH:MM) |  | 5 | `19:00` | 24h format. End before start = overnight window |
| `active` | Boolean | Active |  |  | `true` | Kill switch: unchecked slideshows never play |
| `public` | Boolean | Public |  |  | `false` | When checked, anyone can open this slideshow (bypasses the creator/service-account access check) |
| `player_link` | Url | Player link |  | 512 |  | Direct link to this slideshow on its screen — maintained automatically from the service account |
| `refresh_interval` | Integer | Refresh interval (s) |  |  | `60` | How often the player polls for changes |

_accessibleFrom: package_private · actions: create, read, update, delete · audit · Table API access_
<!-- END:generated:tables -->

## REST API

<!-- BEGIN:generated:rest -->
### ODM Player — base `/api/x_804244_odm/player`

ODM player API — a single route: the deck JSON the player template polls.

| Method | Path | Produces | Handler | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `GET` | `/api/x_804244_odm/player/deck` | application/json | `serveDeckJson` | Deck JSON for the player to poll |
<!-- END:generated:rest -->

## Business rules

<!-- BEGIN:generated:business-rules -->
| Rule | Table | When | Actions | Order | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ODM: unique active slideshow per account | `x_804244_odm_slideshow` | before | insert, update | 100 | Rejects a second active slideshow for the same service account. The player resolves exactly one deck per screen. |
| ODM: maintain player link | `x_804244_odm_slideshow` | before | insert, update | 200 | Sets the read-only player_link field from the assigned service account |
<!-- END:generated:business-rules -->

## Access control (ACLs)

<!-- BEGIN:generated:acls -->
| Table | Operation | Type | Admin override | Condition / script | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `x_804244_odm_slideshow` | create | record | yes | `answer = gs.isLoggedIn();` | Any authenticated user can create a slideshow |
| `x_804244_odm_slideshow` | read | record | yes | `sys_created_by=javascript:gs.getUserName()^ORassigned_account=javascript:gs.getUserID()` | Creator or assigned service account can read the slideshow |
| `x_804244_odm_slideshow` | write | record | yes | `sys_created_by=javascript:gs.getUserName()` | Only the creator can edit the slideshow |
| `x_804244_odm_slideshow` | delete | record | yes | `sys_created_by=javascript:gs.getUserName()` | Only the creator can delete the slideshow |
<!-- END:generated:acls -->

## Forms

<!-- BEGIN:generated:forms -->
### `x_804244_odm_slideshow` form

Default form for Slideshow: identity + assignment on top (with the direct player link), content and timing below.

- **Slideshow**: `name`, `assigned_account`, `active`, `public`, `player_link`, `slide_duration`, `refresh_interval`, `links`, `description`
- **Working hours**: `hours_start`, `hours_end`

| `parseLinks(raw)` | Parse the `links` field into slide URLs. |

| `buildDeck(screen, fields)` | Build the deck object served to the player. |
| `escapeDeckJson(deck)` | Serialize a deck for injection into the HTML template. |
| `injectDeck(templateHtml, deck)` | Inject the deck into the template HTML. |

| Export | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `renderPlayerHtml(screen)` | Server-side render for the direct UI page (/x_804244_odm_player.do): the browser gets the finished document, nothing client-side. |
| `serveDeckJson(request, response)` | GET /player/deck?screen=... |

## Testing

<!-- BEGIN:manual:testing -->
Two layers, so most coverage is fast and instance-free:

- **Unit tests (Jest)** cover the pure logic — the `links` parser, deck building, injection escaping, the player rotation/working-hours state machine, and the template generator — plus the Glide handler layer against an in-memory fake (`test/fakes/glide.js`), so no instance is needed and every branch is reachable.
- **End-to-end tests (Playwright)** drive a real browser against a live instance: log in, open the direct player page, and assert the deck renders, slides rotate, and the open-time access check allows the creator / service account / admin (and `public` decks) while denying strangers. Fixtures (a disposable service account + slideshow) are created and torn down automatically; secrets come from a gitignored `.env` (`ODM_ADMIN_PASS`, `ODM_STRANGER_PASS`, …).

The table below is generated from the test sources.
<!-- END:manual:testing -->

<!-- BEGIN:generated:tests -->
**Unit (Jest):** 102 cases across 4 files — run `npm test`. **End-to-end (Playwright):** 3 cases against a live instance — run `npm run test:e2e`. (Counts derived from source; `test.each` rows counted individually.)

### Unit tests (Jest)

| File | Cases | Suites |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `test/deck.test.ts` | 21 | parseLinks · toPositiveInt · buildDeck · escapeDeckJson / injectDeck |
| `test/handlers.test.ts` | 18 | access control (creator / service account / admin, else denied) · serveDeckJson resolution · renderPlayerHtml (direct UI page — the only HTML surface) · session token (the deck poll carries X-UserToken) |
| `test/player.test.js` | 51 | parseHHMM · isWithinHours · nextIndex · normalizeDeck · resolveState · fingerprint has no separator-collision ambiguity · session token pass-through · addCacheBuster · deckChanged |
| `test/template-lib.test.js` | 12 | inlinePlayerScript · stripLocalImports · generateRoutesModule |

### End-to-end tests (Playwright)

| File | Cases | Suites |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `test/e2e/player.spec.ts` | 3 | ODM player (live instance) |
<!-- END:generated:tests -->
